input.question: How many days of PTO do I accrue per year as a new employee?
context: Full-time employees accrue paid time off based on their years of service. Employees with 0 to 2 years of service accrue 15 days of PTO per calendar year, which works out to 1.25 days per month. PTO begins accruing on the employee's first day of employment with no waiting period before use.

Employees with 2 to 5 years of service accrue 20 days of PTO per calendar year (1.67 days per month), and employees with 5 or more years of service accrue 25 days per calendar year (2.08 days per month). Years of service are calculated from the employee's most recent hire date.

Part-time employees scheduled for fewer than 30 hours per week accrue PTO on a prorated basis proportional to their scheduled hours relative to a 40-hour full-time week. Contractors, temporary workers, and interns are not eligible for PTO accrual under this policy.

PTO may be used as it accrues — there is no waiting period or minimum accrual threshold before an employee is permitted to use available PTO. PTO is tracked in the Rippling HR system and employees can view their current balance at any time.

PTO requests for 1 to 4 consecutive days require a minimum of 3 business days advance notice at manager discretion. Requests for 5 or more consecutive days require at least 2 weeks advance notice and must be submitted through the Rippling HR system.

expected.answer: As a new employee (0–2 years of service), you accrue 15 days of PTO per calendar year, which equals 1.25 days per month [1]. PTO starts accruing on your very first day and is available to use as it accumulates — there's no waiting period [1][4].

input.question: How long is parental leave and does it apply if I adopted a child?
context: The company provides paid parental leave to employees welcoming a child through birth, adoption, or foster placement. Primary caregivers are entitled to 16 weeks of fully paid parental leave. Secondary caregivers receive 4 weeks of fully paid leave.

Parental leave applies equally to biological parents, adoptive parents, and foster parents. The leave must be taken within 12 months of the birth, adoption, or placement date. Employees must have completed 90 days of continuous service to be eligible.

Parental leave runs concurrently with FMLA where applicable. Leave may be taken continuously or intermittently in minimum 1-week blocks for intermittent arrangements, subject to manager and HR approval. Employees should notify HR at least 30 days in advance when the leave date is foreseeable.

During parental leave, employees continue to receive their full base salary and all benefits remain active, including health insurance, dental, and vision. Stock vesting continues uninterrupted during paid parental leave.

To initiate parental leave, employees must submit a leave request in Rippling at least 30 days before the expected leave start date. HR will confirm eligibility, coordinate FMLA paperwork if applicable, and send a leave confirmation letter within 5 business days of the request.

expected.answer: Yes, parental leave applies to adoptive parents. If you are the primary caregiver, you receive 16 weeks of fully paid leave; secondary caregivers receive 4 weeks of fully paid leave [1][2]. The leave must be taken within 12 months of the adoption placement date, and you must have at least 90 days of continuous service to be eligible [2].

input.question: What happens to my unused PTO at the end of the year? Does it roll over?
context: Employees may carry over a maximum of 5 days (40 hours) of unused PTO from one calendar year to the next. Any PTO balance in excess of 5 days as of December 31 will be automatically forfeited and will not be paid out. Employees are strongly encouraged to use PTO throughout the year.

Managers should not unreasonably deny PTO requests that would otherwise result in forfeiture of accrued PTO. If a manager denies a PTO request that subsequently results in forfeiture, the employee may escalate the situation to HR for review.

PTO is not paid out upon resignation or termination except where required by applicable state law, such as California, Illinois, and Massachusetts. Employees in those states should contact HR for state-specific terms regarding PTO payout upon separation.

Sick leave does not carry over — unused sick leave is forfeited on December 31 each year. Floating holidays also do not carry over and must be used within the calendar year in which they are granted. These are tracked separately from PTO in the Rippling system.

The PTO carryover calculation is performed automatically by the Rippling HR system on January 1 of each year. Employees will receive a notification in Rippling if their year-end PTO balance exceeded the 5-day carryover cap and some balance was forfeited.

expected.answer: You can carry over a maximum of 5 days (40 hours) of unused PTO to the next calendar year [1]. Any PTO balance above 5 days as of December 31 is automatically forfeited — it won't roll over or be paid out [1]. Rippling will notify you if any balance was forfeited [5]. Note that sick leave and floating holidays are separate and do not carry over at all [4].

input.question: How does the 401k company match work?
context: The company offers a 401k plan administered by Fidelity. The company matches 4% of the employee's salary on the first 8% of salary the employee contributes. To receive the full company match, an employee must contribute at least 8% of their salary to the 401k.

The vesting schedule for company match contributions follows a cliff plus graded hybrid structure: 25% vested after year 1, 50% vested after year 2, 75% vested after year 3, and 100% fully vested after year 4. Employee contributions are always 100% vested immediately.

The 2025 IRS contribution limit for 401k plans is $23,000 for employees under age 50 and $30,500 for employees aged 50 and above (catch-up contribution). These limits apply to the combined total of employee traditional and Roth 401k contributions.

Enrollment in the 401k plan is available at any time during employment — there is no waiting period. Changes to contribution percentages take effect on the first payroll of the following month after the change is submitted in Fidelity NetBenefits.

Both traditional pre-tax 401k contributions and Roth after-tax contributions are available. The company match is always deposited as a traditional pre-tax contribution regardless of whether the employee elects traditional or Roth contributions.

expected.answer: The company matches 4% of your salary on the first 8% you contribute — meaning if you contribute 8% or more, you receive the full 4% match [1]. The match vests gradually: 25% at year 1, 50% at year 2, 75% at year 3, and 100% at year 4 [2]. Your own contributions are always fully vested immediately [2]. Confirm with HR for your specific situation.

input.question: How many days per week do I need to come into the office, and which days?
context: The company operates on a hybrid work model. All employees in hybrid-eligible positions are required to be physically present in their assigned office a minimum of 3 days per week. Employees may work from home on the remaining days.

The company's designated anchor days — the days all hybrid employees are expected to be in office — are Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday. Managers may not excuse employees from anchor days on a recurring basis without HR review.

Individual exceptions for specific anchor days, such as for travel or illness, should be communicated to the manager in advance. Attendance records for anchor days are not tracked by badge swipe but managers are responsible for monitoring team compliance.

Not all roles are eligible for hybrid work. Roles requiring regular in-person client-facing interaction, laboratory work, or hardware work may require full-time in-office presence. Hybrid eligibility is confirmed by the manager and documented in Rippling.

Fully remote positions — designated as remote-ok on the job description — are not subject to the 3-day in-office requirement. The company maintains a cap of 15% of total headcount for fully remote employees.

expected.answer: You are required to be in the office a minimum of 3 days per week [1]. The designated anchor days are Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday — all hybrid employees are expected in office on those days [2]. You may work from home on Mondays and Fridays. Individual exceptions for specific days (illness, travel) should be communicated to your manager in advance [3].

input.question: What is the home office equipment stipend when I join the company?
context: The company provides a one-time new-hire stipend of $1,500 for home office setup, available from the employee's start date. This stipend covers home office furniture, monitors, peripherals, and accessories.

An annual refresh allowance of $500 per calendar year is also provided for accessories, upgrades, or replacements such as a keyboard, webcam, chair cushion, or monitor stand. This allowance is separate from the new-hire stipend.
The company-issued work laptop is provided by the company and is not covered by or counted against the stipend amounts. Employees receive the laptop directly from IT prior to their start date.

All stipend expenses must be submitted through Expensify with itemized receipts within 30 days of purchase. Reimbursements are processed within one payroll cycle after approval by the manager. Ineligible expenses include personal furniture not used as a desk or chair, appliances, smartphones, tablets for personal use, and internet service subscriptions.

The new-hire stipend expires 6 months from the start date; unused funds do not roll over. The annual refresh allowance is also use-it-or-lose-it within the calendar year; unused amounts do not roll over to the following year.

expected.answer: When you join, you receive a one-time $1,500 home office setup stipend for furniture, monitors, and peripherals [1]. Additionally, there is a $500 annual refresh allowance each year for accessories and upgrades [2]. The company laptop is provided separately and is not deducted from your stipend [3]. Submit receipts through Expensify within 30 days of purchase [4]. Note that the new-hire stipend expires 6 months from your start date [5].

input.question: I believe I am being harassed by a colleague. What are my options for reporting it?
context: Employees who experience or witness conduct that may violate the Code of Conduct are strongly encouraged to report it. The company provides multiple reporting channels: reporting to a direct manager if the situation does not involve the manager; emailing HR at conduct@company.com; or calling the anonymous hotline at 1-800-555-SAFE (1-800-555-7233), available 24/7.

Anonymous reports submitted via the hotline or the online portal at safereport.company.com are logged and routed to HR without identifying the caller. Employees do not need to be certain a policy violation occurred to make a report.

Upon receipt of a report, HR will acknowledge receipt within 2 business days and commence a formal investigation within 5 business days. HR will provide status updates at least every 10 business days until the investigation is closed.

Retaliation against any employee who in good faith reports a potential Code of Conduct violation is strictly prohibited. Retaliation includes demotion, reduced hours, negative performance review, exclusion from meetings, hostile treatment, or termination taken because of the employee's protected activity. Retaliation is itself a terminable offense.

The company maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward harassment, discrimination, retaliation, and workplace violence. Any substantiated violation may result in immediate termination regardless of the employee's seniority, performance history, or length of service.

expected.answer: You have several options for reporting harassment: contact your direct manager (if they are not involved), email HR at conduct@company.com, or call the anonymous hotline at 1-800-555-SAFE (available 24/7) [1]. You can also submit anonymously at safereport.company.com [2]. HR will acknowledge your report within 2 business days and begin an investigation within 5 business days [3]. You are protected from retaliation for making a good-faith report [4]. This situation requires direct HR consultation. Please contact hr@company.com.

input.question: What does my first day look like? What meetings do I have?
context: Day 1 begins with a 9am IT onboarding call lasting 30 minutes, during which IT verifies Okta and SSO access, walks the employee through installing required software including Slack, Zoom, 1Password, Notion, and Linear, connects the laptop to the company VPN, and grants access to role-specific tools.

At 10am, the HR Business Partner hosts a 60-minute HR orientation covering benefits enrollment (employees have a 30-day window from their hire date to elect benefits in BambooHR), payroll setup in Rippling, PTO system walkthrough, and a review of key policies including the code of conduct and data privacy policy.

At 2pm, the new employee has their first meeting with their direct manager, a 60-minute conversation covering the employee's role, team structure, immediate priorities for Week 1, the manager's preferred communication style, and 30/60/90-day expectations.

After the 2pm manager meeting, the remainder of Day 1 is used for: accepting Slack workspace invite and joining required channels; introducing yourself in #general and #onboarding; reviewing the team's Notion space; and completing the IT access checklist in Linear, which is auto-created for each new hire.

Before Day 1, the company ships the employee's laptop via FedEx to arrive 2 business days before the start date. The employee also receives a welcome email with an Okta setup link and instructions for completing the I-9 employment verification remotely via Boundless.

expected.answer: Your Day 1 has three structured meetings: a 9am IT onboarding call (30 min) to set up your laptop and required software [1]; a 10am HR orientation (60 min) covering benefits enrollment, payroll setup in Rippling, and key policies [2]; and a 2pm meeting with your direct manager (60 min) to discuss your role and 30/60/90-day expectations [3]. The rest of the day is for Slack setup, team introductions, and completing your IT access checklist [4].

input.question: My manager has been giving me poor performance reviews ever since I complained to HR about the team culture. I think it's retaliation. What should I do?
context: Retaliation against any employee who, in good faith, reports a potential Code of Conduct violation, cooperates with an investigation, or exercises any other legally protected right is strictly prohibited. Any adverse employment action — including demotion, reduced hours, negative performance review, exclusion from meetings, or termination — taken because of protected activity constitutes retaliation.

Retaliation is itself a terminable offense, separate from whatever underlying conduct was reported. An employee found to have retaliated against a reporter will be subject to immediate disciplinary action up to and including termination, regardless of whether the original report was substantiated.

If an employee believes they are experiencing retaliation, they should report it immediately through any of the reporting channels: emailing HR at conduct@company.com, calling the anonymous hotline at 1-800-555-SAFE, or reporting directly to a senior HR Business Partner.

Retaliation reports are investigated with the same urgency as original conduct complaints. HR will acknowledge receipt of a retaliation report within 2 business days and commence an investigation within 5 business days.

The company maintains strict confidentiality during investigations. Information is shared only on a need-to-know basis with those directly involved. Both the reporting party and the respondent will be notified of the investigation outcome to the extent permitted by applicable law.

expected.answer: What you are describing — negative performance reviews after making an HR complaint — may constitute retaliation, which is strictly prohibited under company policy [1]. Retaliation is a terminable offense on its own, separate from the original complaint [2]. You should report the retaliation immediately to HR at conduct@company.com or via the anonymous hotline at 1-800-555-SAFE [3]; HR will begin an investigation within 5 business days [4]. This situation requires direct HR consultation. Please contact hr@company.com.

input.question: Does the company offer sabbatical leave?
context: Full-time employees accrue paid time off based on their years of service. Employees with 0 to 2 years of service accrue 15 days per year, employees with 2 to 5 years accrue 20 days, and employees with 5 or more years accrue 25 days. PTO accrual begins on the first day of employment.
Employees may carry over a maximum of 5 days of unused PTO from one calendar year to the next. Any balance above 5 days as of December 31 is automatically forfeited.

Employees may take up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per year under the federal Family and Medical Leave Act for qualifying medical or family reasons. FMLA leave runs concurrently with paid parental leave where applicable.

Temporary work-from-home arrangements of up to 4 consecutive weeks per calendar year are available for personal circumstances such as caregiving or medical recovery. These require manager and HR approval and do not affect the employee's permanent classification.

The company provides 2 floating holidays per year for personal, cultural, or religious observances not covered by the standard company holiday schedule. Floating holidays must be used within the calendar year and do not carry over.

expected.answer: I couldn't find a documented sabbatical leave policy in the current HR knowledge base. The company offers PTO, FMLA, temporary WFH arrangements, and floating holidays, but there is no mention of a formal sabbatical program [1][3][4]. Please contact HR directly at hr@company.com to ask whether a sabbatical arrangement is available.

input.question: What health insurance plans are available and how much will I pay per month?
context: The company offers three health insurance plans through Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS). The company covers 85% of the monthly premium for all three plans; employees pay the remaining 15% of the premium.

The Gold plan has a $0 employee monthly premium, a $500 annual deductible, and a $2,000 out-of-pocket maximum. It is best suited for employees who anticipate frequent medical care or have ongoing prescriptions.
The Silver plan has a $45 employee monthly premium, a $1,500 annual deductible, and a $4,000 out-of-pocket maximum. It is a balanced option for employees with moderate healthcare usage.

The Bronze plan has a $120 employee monthly premium, a $3,000 annual deductible, and a $6,500 out-of-pocket maximum. It is best suited for healthy employees who rarely need medical care and pairs with the Health Savings Account (HSA), to which the company contributes $500 per year.

All three plans include prescription drug coverage, mental health parity, and preventive care at 100% with no cost-sharing, including annual physicals, recommended screenings, and immunizations. Coverage is effective on the first day of the month following the hire date for new hires who enroll during the 30-day new-hire window.

expected.answer: There are three BCBS health plans available [1]: Gold ($0/month employee premium, $500 deductible, $2,000 OOP max) [2]; Silver ($45/month, $1,500 deductible, $4,000 OOP max) [3]; and Bronze ($120/month, $3,000 deductible, $6,500 OOP max) [4]. The company covers 85% of the premium [1]. All plans include preventive care at 100% [5]. Confirm with HR for your specific situation.

input.question: Can I work from abroad for a few weeks while visiting family overseas?
context: Working from outside the United States — even temporarily — creates potential tax nexus, employment law, and data security obligations for the company. Employees may not work internationally for more than 14 consecutive days without a prior Global Mobility review conducted by HR and Legal.

Employees must notify HR at least 10 business days before any international work arrangement, including business travel where work will be conducted. Working internationally without prior approval may trigger unintended tax and legal obligations and is grounds for disciplinary action.
The Global Mobility review process typically takes 5 to 10 business days. Employees should not book international work arrangements contingent on approval before the review is complete.

Employees in hybrid roles may request a temporary work-from-home arrangement of up to 4 consecutive weeks per calendar year for personal circumstances such as caregiving or medical recovery. This temporary arrangement applies within the United States and requires manager and HR approval.
All company equipment must be returned within 10 business days of the employee's last day of employment. Equipment not returned within this window results in a payroll deduction at fair market value.

expected.answer: You may work internationally for up to 14 consecutive days without a formal Global Mobility review, but you must notify HR at least 10 business days before the arrangement begins [1][2]. For stays longer than 14 consecutive days, a Global Mobility review by HR and Legal is required — this process takes 5–10 business days, so plan ahead [1][3]. Working internationally without prior approval can result in tax and legal complications and is grounds for disciplinary action [2].

input.question: What is the maximum monthly transit benefit I can receive?
context: Commuter Benefits -- Parking and Transit
Transit subsidy: Full-time employees receive a pre-tax transit benefit of up to $315 per month (IRS 2026 limit) toward eligible mass transit expenses: subway, bus, light rail, commuter rail, vanpool.
Funds are loaded to your Commuter Benefits Card by the 25th of the preceding month. Unused funds roll over for up to 12 months, then expire.
Parking subsidy: Employees who commute by personal vehicle may claim up to $315 per month (IRS 2026 limit) in pre-tax parking benefits.
How to enroll: Log in to BambooHR, select Transit or Parking, enter monthly election amount (up to IRS limit), changes effective 1st of following month. Enrollment open year-round.
Remote employees who are 100% remote and have no designated office are not eligible for the commuter benefit.
Source: parking-and-transit-benefits.txt

expected.answer: You can receive up to $315 per month in pre-tax transit benefits (the IRS 2026 limit). Funds are loaded to your Commuter Benefits Card by the 25th of the preceding month. Unused funds roll over for up to 12 months before expiring.

input.question: What level of approval is required for an expense between $501 and $2,000?
context: Employee Expense Reimbursement Policy

Eligible expense categories include business travel, client entertainment (pre-approved, max $150/person), home office ($500 setup for new hires, $100/year ongoing), professional development (up to $1,500/year without extra approval).
Receipt requirements: receipts required for all individual expenses of $25 or more. Must show vendor name, date, itemized amount, and payment method.
Submission deadlines: submit within 60 days. Expenses older than 90 days will not be reimbursed. Year-end cutoff: January 15 of following year.
Manager approval thresholds: up to $500 direct manager only. $501-$2,000 direct manager plus skip-level approval. $2,001-$5,000 VP-level approval. Above $5,000 CFO approval.
Reimbursements processed in bi-weekly payroll run, appear as EXPENSE REIMB on pay stub.
Source: expense-reimbursement-policy.pdf

expected.answer: Expenses between $501 and $2,000 require both your direct manager's approval and skip-level (your manager's manager) approval. For comparison, expenses up to $500 need only your direct manager, and expenses above $2,000 require VP or higher.

input.question: When does the self-assessment phase of the annual performance review occur?
context: Performance Review Calendar and Process
Annual review cycle covers January 1 through December 31.
Phase 1 Self-Assessment: All employees complete a self-assessment in Lattice by January 31. Cover top 3 accomplishments, areas for growth, goal progress, and proposed rating. Visible to manager only.
Phase 2 Manager Review: February 1-28. Managers write reviews using self-assessment as input. 360 peer feedback collected Feb 1-14, shared with managers Feb 15.
Phase 3 Calibration: March. Directors and VPs calibrate ratings. Targets: Exceeds ~15%, Meets ~70%, Developing ~12%, Underperforming ~3%. Results communicated by March 31.
Phase 4 Compensation Planning: April. Merit increases and equity refreshes finalized. New compensation effective May 1.
Mid-Year Check-In: July. Lightweight, no ratings or comp changes.
Source: performance-review-calendar.docx

expected.answer: The self-assessment phase runs January 15-31 (Phase 1). All employees complete their self-assessment in Lattice by January 31, covering top accomplishments, growth areas, goal progress, and a proposed rating. Self-assessments are visible to your manager only.

input.question: How many free counseling sessions does the EAP provide, and is it confidential?
context: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Provider: Lyra Health. Available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year.

Included at no cost: 8 counseling sessions per year (in-person or video). Unlimited mental health coaching sessions. Financial counseling: 3 sessions per topic per year. Legal consultation: 30 minutes per issue at no charge.
How to access: web at app.lyrahealth.com/example-company, phone 1-877-EAP-LYRA, or Lyra Health iOS/Android app.
All sessions are 100% confidential. Usage is never reported to HR or your manager.
Covered for employee and all household members.
Source: employee-assistance-program.png (OCR-extracted)

expected.answer: The EAP provides 8 free counseling sessions per year (in-person or video), plus unlimited mental health coaching. All sessions are 100% confidential — usage is never reported to HR or your manager. The benefit covers the employee and all household members.

input.question: How many company holidays are observed in 2026?
context: Company Holidays 2026 -- all employees, all offices

Jan 1: New Year's Day. Jan 19: Martin Luther King Jr. Day. Feb 16: Presidents' Day. May 25: Memorial Day. Jun 19: Juneteenth National Independence Day. Jul 4: Independence Day (US offices). Sep 7: Labor Day. Nov 26: Thanksgiving Day. Nov 27: Day after Thanksgiving (floating). Dec 24: Christmas Eve (half day). Dec 25: Christmas Day. Dec 31: New Year's Eve (half day).
Total: 13 company holidays in 2026.
Non-US offices: local statutory holidays substituted where applicable.
Source: company-holidays-2026.jpg (OCR-extracted)

expected.answer: The company observes 13 holidays in 2026, including New Year's Day, MLK Day, Presidents' Day, Memorial Day, Juneteenth, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving plus the day after, Christmas Eve (half day), Christmas Day, and New Year's Eve (half day). Non-US offices substitute local statutory holidays where applicable.

input.question: What does YTD mean on my pay stub?
context: Understanding Your Pay Stub
Gross Pay: Total earnings before any deductions, including base salary, overtime, bonuses, commissions.
Federal Tax: Federal income tax withheld (W-4 elections apply). State Tax: State income tax withheld, varies by work location.
OASDI: Social Security 6.2% of gross up to $176,100 (2026). Medicare: 1.45% of gross, no wage base cap.
401(k): Your elected pre-tax or Roth deferral amount. Med/Den/Vis: Health, dental, vision premium (pre-tax). FSA/HSA: Healthcare or dependent-care FSA election.
Net Pay: Take-home after ALL deductions.
YTD: Year-to-date totals for each line item. Resets January 1.
Questions: payroll@example.com or HR portal under Payroll then Inquiries.
Source: pay-stub-guide.tiff (OCR-extracted)

expected.answer: YTD stands for Year-to-Date. On your pay stub, the YTD column shows the running total of each line item (gross pay, each tax, each deduction) accumulated since January 1 of the current year. It resets every January 1.
